title: uvaria littoralis
intro: Uvaria littoralis, also known as sea poisonwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae. It is native to tropical coastal regions in the Pacific Islands and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Uvaria littoralis include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves can be used to make a paste or ointment for topical application.
